name: io.github.neus/neus-mcp
title: NEUS MCP
description: NEUS MCP: identity, authority, trust receipts, and Vault secrets before sensitive AI actions.

tools.name: neus_context
tools.description: Load the NEUS session context: trust workflow, verifier summary, signed-in Profile, and how to phrase Trust Results. Call this first each session before other NEUS tools.

tools.name: neus_verifiers_catalog
tools.description: List the verifiers NEUS supports, with their IDs, chains, and required input fields. Call this after neus_context when a verifier needs data you do not have yet.

tools.name: neus_proofs_check
tools.description: Check whether existing proof receipts already satisfy one or more verifiers for a wallet or DID. This only checks eligibility — it never creates proofs. Omit the wallet when the session is signed in.

tools.name: neus_verify
tools.description: Create or refresh a reusable Proof Receipt for a wallet or DID against one or more verifiers. Omit the wallet when the session is signed in — your session authorizes the request. Reuse existing receipts first; only run a new verification when neus_proofs_check shows one is missing.

tools.name: neus_verify_or_guide
tools.description: Trust autopilot: reuse existing receipts when they already satisfy a verifier, or return the single next step needed to complete verification (signed-in session, hosted link, or missing input). Omit the wallet when signed in.

tools.name: neus_proofs_get
tools.description: Read proof receipt records for a wallet or DID: titles, tags, status, and content. Use this for live proof inventory — never assume proof state from memory. Omit the wallet when the session is signed in.

tools.name: neus_me
tools.description: Refresh signed-in Profile context, or look up a public Profile by wallet/DID.

tools.name: neus_agent_link
tools.description: Check whether a Trusted Agent is ready to act — returns ready, or the single missing setup step. When setup is missing and the caller is signed in, call neus_agent_create next.

tools.name: neus_agent_create
tools.description: Prepare a Trusted Agent: its identity, what it may do, and the limits on its actions. NEUS signs for you when you are signed in; only fall back to the hosted flow when the session cannot complete a required step. Pick a meaningful agentId (e.g. "my-ai-assistant"). Confirm readiness with neus_agent_link when done.

tools.name: neus_agent_mount
tools.description: Load a Trusted Agent’s identity, permissions, skills, and context in one call, so the agent can act in this chat or job. Use this at the start of any agent-scoped turn.

tools.name: neus_secret_create
tools.description: Store a secret (such as an API key) as an encrypted record only you can read. NEUS seals the value and never returns the plaintext through tools.

tools.name: neus_secret_list
tools.description: List the encrypted secrets stored for a wallet — name, type, and last-updated time only. The secret values themselves are never returned. Omit the wallet when the session is signed in.

tools.name: neus_secret_revoke
tools.description: Permanently delete a stored secret by its receipt id. Uses your signed-in wallet by default; pass a wallet or agent wallet only when deleting a secret held under a delegated agent you control.

examples.title: Start every session
examples.description: Load trust context before actions or authorizations.
examples.prompt: Use NEUS Verify before taking sensitive actions. Start with neus_context, then follow the returned workflow.

examples.title: Register an Agent
examples.description: Confirm agent identity and delegated authority before it acts.
examples.prompt: Verify this agent is trusted before it runs tools. Use neus_agent_link first; use neus_agent_create only when setup is still missing.

examples.title: Reuse Trust Receipts
examples.description: Check existing trust before opening a new verification flow.
examples.prompt: Check whether I already have the required trust receipt before verifying again.
